title: TCG Day 2 | 2026 Pokémon Prague Regional Championships
round: Asymmetrical Top Cut — Top 16 (winner advances to Top 8)
transcript_source: yt-dlp auto-subs (en) — segment ~02:25:30 to 03:05:01 of the 8h11m broadcast

p1.decklist_summary: Dragapult ex / Drakloak / Dreepy core + a 2-2-1 split Dudunsparce (Run-Away Draw nonex line) and 1 Dudunsparce ex (Tenacious Tail — 60×opponent ex Pokémon, Shred ignores effects). Munkidori (Adrena-Brain). Crispin, Lillie's Determination, Buddy-Buddy Poffin, Ultra Ball, Poké Pad, Boss's Orders, Rare Candy. ACE SPEC: Hero's Cape (+100 HP — caster called this 'spicy choice over the more common Unfair Stamp'). One Budew (only copy, prized in Game 1).

p2.decklist_summary: Maman's Grimmsnarl ex (Shadow Bullet 180+30 bench, Punk Up energy-acceleration ability), Maman's Morgrem, Maman's Impidimp, Maman's Tatsugiri (Order Up — top-6 supporter tutor), Frosmoth/Frostlass (Freezing Shroud — checkup damage to abilities), Munkidori, Snorunt. Spike Muff Gym stadium (search any Maman Pokémon — item-lock immune). ACE SPEC: Unfair Stamp. Trainers: Lillie's Determination, Crispin, Team Rocket's Petrel (Order Up tutor target), Boss's Orders, Buddy-Buddy Poffin, Pokégear 3.0.

coin_flip.notes: Mateusz higher seed (top-cut rule: higher seed chooses)

match_outcome.advances_to: Top 8 (vs winner of Tor vs Elmar bracket — turned out to be Tord Reklev's bracket but Tor lost top 8)

games.win_condition: concession (Nicklas scooped after Mateusz built a multi-prize threat with Boss's Orders + Phantom Dive sweeping monkey-doried Pokémon)

actions.notes: fetched Dreepy + Munkidori; Munkidori benched alongside an existing Dreepy
tutored a Drakloak into hand

turns.notes: Standard Dragapult-Dudunsparce going-first opener: Poffin to fan out the Dreepys + Munkidori, Poké Pad for Drakloak prep, no T1 supporter (legal exceptions don't apply). No Budew (only copy is prized) — caster: 'guaranteed not coming this turn.'

actions.notes: fetched Tatsugiri + Snorunt (caster: 'fantastic start … double Buddy Buddy Poffin')
second Poffin — fetched Maman's Impidimp + Budew
free retreat (caster: 'free retreat into the Tatsugiri')

actions.effect: look at top 6 of deck, take 1 supporter — picked Team Rocket's Petrel

actions.notes: passed without Itchy Pollen — Nicklas chose Petrel-into-Lillie's bank over T2 item-lock; caster flagged this as a clear strategic choice given no Itchy Pollen window vs Dragapult-Dudunsparce

turns.notes: Tatsugiri Order Up engine setting up a future supporter chain. Caster commentary: 'prioritized finding a supporter for the Lillie's no matter what.'

actions.notes: discarded 2 cards, fetched a Drakloak (now 2 in hand)
shuffle hand into deck, draw 8 — strong T3 (caster: 'best supporter in the game right now')
no evolutions yet this turn — Mateusz banking attachments behind the Dreepy active
shuffle + draw 8 — supporter chain off the Petrel/Order Up line
stadium — searches any Maman Pokémon when needed; item-lock immune
caster: 'we are going to see Freezing Shroud Frostlass hit the field. We are going to see Maman's Morgrem hit the field.'
needed to retreat Tatsugiri before attacking

actions.effect: 20 base × 2 (weakness Dark→Psychic) + 10 from Freezing Shroud, defender retreat-locked
actions.notes: caster: 'weak to dark, so 40 + 10 from Freezing Shroud = 50 damage on the active'

turns.notes: Itchy Pollen unavailable (no item-lock window). Clutch retreat-lock chosen because Mateusz has no out — 'he's prioritizing clutch knowing it's going to give him another turn.'

actions.notes: tutored 2 different basic energies — attached Psychic to active, Fire-or-Psychic to hand. Mateusz did NOT promote a Mind Bend (60 + Confusion) attacker to break Clutch lock — caster: 'we're not going to be seeing a mind bend.'
Hero's Cape pulled into hand off Recon Directive

turns.notes: Crispin played for energy, NOT Mind Bend break — Mateusz absorbed another Clutch turn rather than coin-flip the lock break. Caster: 'I would have minded the mind bend but it looks like recon directive now.'

Approximately 02:36–02:50 transcript focuses on caster discussion and an interview. Game state visible in next clean window: Nicklas had Frostlass + Grimmsnarl line set up; Mateusz had Drakloak evolved and was preparing the Phantom Dive line.

actions.notes: 'Matt finishing things off there with the Boss's Orders' — gusted a damaged Munkidori onto the active for a multi-prize takedown
caster (recap): 'just clinically dissected Nicholas's boards … picking off these Pokémon slowly but surely'

turns.notes: Caster recap: 'questionable benching from Nicholas ended up biting him … little Budew came back to bite him for a four-five prize'

games.notes: Game 1 driven by Phantom Dive spread on Nicklas's wide rule-box bench. Heroes Cape on Dragapult kept Mateusz's main attacker out of OHKO range from Shadow Bullet (180), forcing Nicklas to whiff multi-turn KO math.

games.win_condition: prizes / concession — caster recap: 'Nicholas adapted, set up a wide board of Munkidori, but Matteusz counter-adapted and just did not — was laser focused on targeting down his Munkidori'

turns.notes: Nicklas chose to go FIRST in Game 2 (loser of Game 1 chooses). 3 mulligans (no Basic) before drawing Snorunt — Mateusz drew 3 extra cards.

actions.notes: stadium — searched a Maman Pokémon (caster: 'Snorunt active, bench Maman's Impidimp, Spike Muff Gym which we can use')
tutored a non-rule-box Pokémon to bench — Munkidori
caster: 'fantastic turn one going first … Munkidori benched immediately gets a dark energy on it'

turns.notes: Mateusz starts +3 cards from mulligans (~10 in hand)

actions.notes: fetched Dreepy
caster: 'turn attach to the Dreepy. That definitely means he hasn't got Budew — normally you'd attach to the active so you can retreat'

turns.notes: Mateusz signaled no-Budew via energy attach pattern (attached to bench Dreepy not active).

actions.notes: shuffle + draw 8 — Mateusz's hand exploded: Buddy-Buddy Poffin, Munkidori, Poké Pad, additional Dreepy, Drakloak — caster: 'he's off to the races'

turns.notes: Game 2 inflection: Mateusz's Lillie hit perfectly. Three Dreepy on bench by end of turn.

games.key_recap: Caster recap: 'Munkidori were not long for this world. They were immediately getting sent to the discard pile. Mateusz with his tricky ace spec of choice — that Hero's Cape was just pushing that Dragapult out of range. That could have been a very different game had it not been for the Hero's Cape.'

key_moments.description: Nicklas chose Tatsugiri Order Up → Petrel → Lillie's bank over T2 Itchy Pollen. Recognized item-lock window had no value vs Mateusz's no-pivot board (no switch, no Budew available next turn from Mateusz's POV).
key_moments.engine_lesson: scorePosition / aiArchetype.ts has no model for 'value of item-lock when opp lacks a pivot' vs 'value of supporter banking'. Order Up is Maman's archetype-defining engine and should be high-priority in a future grimmsnarl-froslass playbook.

key_moments.description: Mateusz played Crispin under Munkidori Clutch retreat-lock but did NOT switch to a Mind Bend (60 + Confusion attacker) to coin-flip-break the lock — chose to absorb another Clutch turn for setup tempo instead.
key_moments.engine_lesson: v2 ai.ts does not currently weight 'Mind Bend lock break' vs 'absorbing a turn' — typically the engine would force the lock-break for any % chance. Real human valued tempo over the 50/50. This is Phase-9 self-tuning territory: lower the priority on coin-flip lock breaks when a clean setup turn is available.

key_moments.description: Mateusz's Hero's Cape on Dragapult ex (320 + 100 = 420 effective HP) put Dragapult out of two-shot range from Maman's Grimmsnarl ex Shadow Bullet (180 × 2 = 360). Caster called this the swing card.
key_moments.engine_lesson: ACE SPEC choice ratification: the engine currently ranks Unfair Stamp as the dominant Dragapult ACE SPEC. Hero's Cape provides defensive HP-buff that wins prize trades against 180-damage attackers. Add 'opp-attacker-OHKO-range bypass' as a Hero's Cape evaluation factor in scorePosition (similar to threat-aware OHKO penalty).

key_moments.description: Nicklas mulliganed THREE TIMES going first, giving Mateusz +3 cards. Mateusz's T2 Lillie's Determination then drew into a perfect 8-card hand and the game was effectively over by T3.
key_moments.engine_lesson: Mulligan-induced extra-card snowball is real but already correctly modeled in setupGame. No engine change needed.

key_moments.description: Mateusz prioritized targeting Munkidori specifically with Phantom Dive spread + KO routing instead of attacking Maman's Grimmsnarl directly. Recognized that without Munkidori, Nicklas can't move damage off his big attackers and the spread becomes lethal.
key_moments.engine_lesson: bestGustTarget in ai.ts already boosts Munkidori (it's in the un-powered rule-box puncher list). Validates the existing weight. Do NOT regress this when adding new entries.

engine_notes: Mateusz's T1 going-first opener (Poffin + Poké Pad + bench Dreepy + Munkidori, no supporter, Psychic to active Dreepy) is the canonical dragapult-dudunsparce going-first line — promote to opening-book.json. Distinct from the dragapult-blaziken playbook because there's no Torchic/Combusken line and no Budew lead.
Hero's Cape is a viable ACE SPEC for Dragapult ex and should be added as a candidate in aiArchetype.ts dragapult lists. Currently the engine treats Unfair Stamp as the only Dragapult ACE SPEC.

Maman's Grimmsnarl-Froslass is a NEW archetype not yet in aiArchetype.ts. Signature cards: Maman's Grimmsnarl ex (Punk Up energy-accel ability), Maman's Tatsugiri (Order Up tutor), Spike Muff Gym (search-Maman stadium, item-lock immune), Frostlass (Freezing Shroud passive ability damage), Munkidori. Suggested archetype string: `grimmsnarl-froslass` or `maman-grimmsnarl`. Playbook: T1 Tatsugiri Order Up → Petrel → bank Lillie's, T2 set up Frostlass + Morgrem, T3 evolve Grimmsnarl ex with pre-attached Dark + Punk Up acceleration.
